title: Midnight Blueprints
lyrics:
[Intro]
Streetlight on the kitchen floor
Kettle hum at 2 a.m.
Window glass is cold as silver
I pull the chair and breathe again
[Verse 1]
Laptop glow on tired hands
Coffee ring on page thirteen
Rain taps sync in quiet triplets
Neon paints the balcony green
I mark one line in cobalt ink
Cross it out and write it true
City noise fades under headphones
Low kick, soft bass, velvet blue
I keep my phone face-down tonight
Let the room become my tide
[Pre-Chorus]
No fireworks, just steady motion
No panic in this pulse of mine
One small step becomes a rhythm
One clean word becomes a line
[Chorus]
Hold me in this afterglow
Hold me in this afterglow
Slow and bright, I keep on moving
Heart and hands in one-time flow
Hold me in this afterglow
Hold me in this afterglow
When the night turns wide and open
I find home inside this glow
[Verse 2]
4:10, first train cuts the dark
Paper cup gone lukewarm now
Neighbor door clicks, hallway whispers
Morning air slips through the house
I fix the bug beneath the bug
Watch red marks turn green in rows
Dawn climbs orange on the monitor
Shoulders drop, the breathing slows
A message blinks: "you still awake?"
I smile and type, "almost done"
[Bridge]
If doubt comes knocking, let it sit
I’ll give it tea, I’ll keep my pace
No need to break to prove I’m trying
Soft can still be made of grace
I came this far on patient timing
I’ll close the file with open lungs
[Final Chorus]
Hold me in this afterglow
Hold me in this afterglow
Now the drums are warm and rising
Golden light on every wall
Hold me in this afterglow
Hold me in this afterglow
I send, I stand, I step into morning
Calm and bright, I have it all
[Outro]
Streetlight fades from kitchen floor
Birdsong where the sirens were
styles: Breathy alto lead with close doubles, stacked 3rd/5th harmonies and final chorus ad-libs; 94 BPM mellow R&B-pop, laid-back groove, dynamics 30% intro to 99% final lift.

title: Polaroid Rain
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
7:12 bus window fogged with names
Thumbprint moon on the glass
Bakery steam and diesel thunder
Red light blinking as we pass
My coat still cold from outside rain
My earbuds warm my left ear
I write a promise in my notes app
"Do one thing and keep it clear"
Office lamp, a honey circle
Mint gum, keys, and coffee stain
I start before the fear can speak
And find my rhythm in the plain
[Chorus]
Take it slow, make it true
Take it slow, make it true
Page by page, the weight gets lighter
Beat by beat, I follow through
Take it slow, make it true
Take it slow, make it true
When the day turns loud and restless
I keep choosing what is true
[Verse 2]
1:58, lunch tray, lemon peel
Projector fan in lazy spin
A text from you says "breathe, you got this"
I grin and pull the chorus in
Cloud shadows drift on meeting glass
Printer heat against my wrist
I cut six words, the line gets cleaner
Simple, sharp, impossible to miss
Traffic draws a silver grid
Across my keyboard and my sleeve
I check off four blue bullet points
And still have room to breathe
[Bridge]
If I lose the thread, I count to four
Name the desk, the cup, the door
No myth of perfect, no race for loud
Just small brave steps inside the storm
I don’t need sparks to call it fire
I don’t need noise to call it art
I only need this steady timing
And a little room inside my heart
[Final Chorus]
Take it slow, make it true
Take it slow, make it true
Now the snare is bright and open
Amber sun on avenue
Take it slow, make it true
Take it slow, make it true
I close the day with quiet power
Walk out glowing, tried and true
[Outro]
Bus stop shining after rain
My reflection smiles back
styles: Airy tenor lead with female harmony layers, hook doubles, tasteful ad-libs in final refrain; 96 BPM indie-pop/R&B blend, tight pocket, dynamic arc from 35% to 100%.

title: Soft Alarm
lyrics:
[Intro]
6:30, blue alarm light
Curtain breathing in the wind
Cold tile, warm socks, half-awake
Another day begins again
[Verse 1]
Toaster clicks and kettle whistles
Notebook open, page still blank
Street below is wet and silver
Taxi tires hiss and thank
I draw a box around one deadline
Circle twice the one that scares
Plant leaves hold last night’s water
Morning dust in sunlit air
I press play on a muted piano
Minor chords beneath my skin
First line rough, second line honest
Third line finally lets me in
[Refrain]
Stay right here, breathe it in
Stay right here, breathe it in
Small and steady, I begin
[Verse 2]
2:22, sparkling water pops
Paper stack beside my hand
Coworker laughs behind the doorway
Orange peel on plastic stand
I rewrite one awkward sentence
Split it into two clear roads
Patch the crack under the headline
Watch the whole frame carry load
A message says, "Dinner tonight?"
I type "yes" and save the file
Sun goes gold on office windows
I hold that color for a while
[Refrain]
Stay right here, hold the light
Stay right here, hold the light
What was heavy now feels right
[Bridge]
When my chest gets tight with rushing
I lower volume, count to four
One for breath and two for posture
Three for grace and four for more
No need to wear a storm to prove it
No need to break to make it true
Quiet focus has a heartbeat
And tonight it carries me through
[Final Refrain]
Stay right here, burn in light
Stay right here, burn in light
Now the chorus blooms around me
City stars in early night
Stay right here, keep this light
Stay right here, keep this light
I finish whole and leave still glowing
Soft alarm turned bright tonight
[Outro]
6:30 far behind me now
Only calm remains
styles: Whispery mezzo lead with intimate doubles, stacked harmonies in final refrain, light call-and-response ad-libs; 90 BPM chill pop-ballad, sparse intro to 98% emotional finale.

title: Late Bus Lullaby
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
8:03, station clock and drizzle
Paper ticket in my sleeve
Coffee steam and wet wool collar
Neon signs that never sleep
I sit by glass and hear the engine
Low and steady under feet
Open drafts from yesterday
Find one phrase that still feels weak
I smooth the edges, keep the center
Cut the glitter, keep the truth
Your voice note says "don’t overthink it"
I laugh and whisper, "copy you"
[Pre-Chorus]
Not every line must be a miracle
Not every hour must be new
If I stay gentle with the process
I get where I need to
[Chorus]
Easy now, carry through
Easy now, carry through
Little sparks in ordinary moments
Turn to something I can use
Easy now, carry through
Easy now, carry through
When the world gets sharp and noisy
I choose what I can carry through
[Verse 2]
3:17, office lights half-dimmed
Printer tray clicks back in place
A bike bell rings beyond the crosswalk
Sunset warming building face
I trace the bug to one wrong comma
Fix the root and test again
Green checks line up like soft lanterns
Breathing deep, unclenching hands
A friend sends "ramen after work?"
I type "yes, five more minutes"
Then I close the longest ticket
And the whole room kind of glitters
[Post-Chorus]
Kick drum low, keys like water
I ride the groove a little farther
[Bridge]
If doubt sits down beside me
I give it space but keep the wheel
No panic badge, no martyr story
Just patient craft and something real
I came here tired, almost brittle
Now I’m quiet, strong, and clear
[Final Chorus]
Easy now, shine it through
Easy now, shine it through
Now the chorus lifts the ceiling
Streetlights bloom in silver blue
Easy now, carry through
Easy now, carry through
I zip my bag and step into evening
Still warm, still open, still true
[Outro]
Last bus doors close softly
My heartbeat keeps the groove
styles: Warm contralto lead with tight doubles, stacked chorus harmonies and restrained final ad-libs; 97 BPM soft urban-pop, syncopated phrasing, dynamics from 30% to full 100% final chorus.

title: Window Seat Hearts
lyrics:
[Intro]
Rain pearls on the subway rail
Earbuds in, world turned low
Finger drawing little circles
On the fogged-up window glow
[Verse 1]
7:25, morning crowd in motion
Leather bags and sleepy eyes
Citrus hand cream, coffee perfume
Gray coats under white tube lights
I open up my notes and type
"Don’t chase perfect, chase alive"
Station names blur into rhythm
Minor chords beneath the ride
At my desk, a yellow mug
Blue sticky notes on monitor side
I choose one lane and stay inside
[Pre-Chorus]
A soft beginning still beginning
A quiet voice still says enough
If I keep breathing with intention
I can turn this into love
[Chorus]
Glow with me, stay with me
Glow with me, stay with me
Hour by hour the day gets kinder
Word by word I set it free
Glow with me, stay with me
Glow with me, stay with me
Even when the clocks are racing
I keep pace with what I need
[Verse 2]
2:40, cold tea on the coaster
Sun stripe crawling up my hand
Coworker hums a distant chorus
Fork and plate in breakroom land
I revise a rough proposal
Swap out ten for only two
Clear and human, less performative
Suddenly the point breaks through
Phone lights up: "How’s your day?"
I send a photo of the sky
Peach and violet on the glass
And a tiny laughing reply
[Bridge]
When pressure rises, I ground my body
Heel to floor and shoulders down
One for breath and two for posture
Three for grace, no need to drown
No cosmic promises, no giant speeches
Just this moment, warm and true
I don’t need louder to be stronger
I just need a little room
[Final Chorus]
Glow with me, burn with me
Glow with me, burn with me
Now the harmony opens wider
City lights like constellations
Glow with me, stay with me
Glow with me, stay with me
I close the file, step into nightfall
Carrying calm I built in me
[Outro]
Rain pearls fade from subway rail
Window seat still knows my heart
styles: Breathy soprano lead with layered doubles, wide stacked harmonies and tasteful final chorus ad-libs; 92 BPM mellow indie-pop, laid-back groove, dynamic rise from intimate 30% intro to 99% finale.
